patterns.name: Ports around infrastructure dependencies
patterns.problemSolved: Keeps application workflow behavior independent from filesystem, process, and runner implementations.
patterns.implementationShape: Application services receive port interfaces and adapters implement Node-specific behavior outside the hexagon.
tradeoffs: Adds interface and adapter plumbing that can feel heavy for very small workflows.
patterns.whenToUse: Use when orchestration needs deterministic tests or replaceable infrastructure.
patterns.whenNotToUse: Avoid when a short one-off script has no meaningful infrastructure variation.
patterns.adaptationValue: Future agents can reuse the port pattern to add new inspection steps without binding the core to Codex or Node APIs.

findings.claim: The workflow uses ports to keep application orchestration independent from concrete adapters.
findings.recommendation: Continue introducing new agent steps through reusable application workflow helpers or ports rather than CLI adapter logic.
